value.body:
Found during a follow-up static issue scan of the Cycle codebase.

Problem:
The desktop renderer accepts API tokens from query/hash parameters and persists them into `window.localStorage`. Bearer tokens in URLs can leak via history, logs, screenshots, and referrers; persisting them in localStorage also makes them long-lived and available to any renderer script running in that origin.

Evidence:
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:136-149` reads `cycleApiToken` / `apiToken` from `window.location.search` and hash query params.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:151-152` writes the token to `API_TOKEN_STORAGE_KEY` in `window.localStorage`.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:160-169` reads that stored token back for later requests.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:293-297` sends the stored token as the bearer Authorization header.

Suggested remediation:
- Do not persist bearer tokens supplied via URL params. Treat URL-provided tokens as one-shot/session-scoped at most.
- Prefer the secure preload bridge (`getApiConnection`) for desktop runtime tokens.
- If browser/dev override tokens are still required, store them in memory or sessionStorage behind explicit dev-only handling and document the risk.
- Provide a migration/cleanup path that removes existing `cycle.api.token` localStorage entries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Query/hash token values are not written to localStorage.
- Existing localStorage token entries are ignored or cleared unless explicitly supported by a documented dev-only mode.
- API discovery tests cover URL token handling without persistent storage.

frontmatter.title: Stop persisting API bearer tokens from URL params into localStorage

value.title: Stop persisting API bearer tokens from URL params into localStorage
